verbatim_text: And you can now sample some of these correct traces. So this revives the learning signal on that hard problem. We can feed this to your RL optimizer. And that should be able to give you a better LLM because now you have some notion of getting to the right answer here. So now we can ask what happens when we train with this surrogate sampler in practice. So first, we look at the case where you only train with the typical sampler. You don't use this offline auxiliary data. You only use standard RL. There we directly sample these online traces and try to reward them. That does learn some LLM that at test time, as you get it to produce more tokens, the accuracy improves reasonably. This is on a contestable math benchmark. But if you were to use the right surrogate sampler, which is the auxiliary data here, then what you do find is that you're able to improve exploration on the hard problems during training so much because now you're able to use a bigger set of them during training. You're able to find some correct answers on a bigger set, which standard RL could not. So you generally get a more performant LLM.
